summary: The implementation covers the vast majority of requirements. All backend functions (REQ-1), navigation (REQ-2), hero section (REQ-3), face detection (REQ-4), AI processing (REQ-5), verification result (REQ-6), access confirmation (REQ-7), member management (REQ-8), entry logs (REQ-9), system status (REQ-10), and premium theme (REQ-11) are present. The hero image path matches REQ-12. A few specific acceptance criteria have gaps or concerns worth flagging.

missing_requirements.requirement: addMember requires admin permission per the backend implementation, but the frontend calls it without authentication context — anonymous callers will likely fail the admin check, meaning the AC 'addMember stores a new member with name and membership tier' may not work in practice for unauthenticated users.
missing_requirements.reason: backend/main.mo gates addMember behind AccessControl.hasPermission(accessControlState, caller, #admin), but the frontend useActor hook creates an anonymous actor for unauthenticated users. This contradicts the non-goal of 'no user authentication' and may prevent the add-member form from working.

missing_requirements.requirement: Entry logs should be stored in the backend and populated on each simulateVerification call. The backend main.mo is truncated before getEntryLogs and the log-writing logic inside simulateVerification are visible, so backend storage AC cannot be confirmed.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend file is truncated before getEntryLogs and log-writing logic in simulateVerification are visible. The frontend hook references actor.getEntryLogs() but its backend implementation cannot be confirmed from the diff.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity authentication integration including InternetIdentityProvider, AuthClient, login/clear functions, and identity-based actor creation.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly lists 'User authentication or Internet Identity' as a non-goal, yet the implementation includes a full InternetIdentityProvider wrapping the app, useInternetIdentity hook, and actor creation tied to identity.

hallucinated_features.description: Access control and authorization system (MixinAuthorization, AccessControl) with admin/user permission gating on all backend functions.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not request any authorization or role-based access control system. The backend imports and uses an authorization mixin that gates addMember to admins and other functions to users, which was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: User profile management functions (getCallerUserProfile, getUserProfile, saveCallerUserProfile) and userProfiles state in the backend.
hallucinated_features.reason: No user profile management was requested in the build request. These functions and state are extra features not specified.

hallucinated_features.description: A face hash input field exposed in the Member Management add-member form.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-8 specifies only name and membership tier fields in the add member form. The implementation adds a third Face Hash input field that was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: caffeine.ai attribution link with UTM tracking parameters in the Footer component.
hallucinated_features.reason: No footer or attribution link was specified in the build request requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: Additional system status indicators beyond the four specified: Network Connectivity, ICP Blockchain Node, and System Activity Monitor.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-10 specifies exactly four indicators: Face Recognition Engine, Database Connection, Camera Feed, and Last System Check. The implementation adds three additional indicators beyond what was specified.
